shorts.title: ​‘The Office US’ cast: Where the Dunder Mifflin folks are now​
shorts.title_distilled: ​‘The Office US’ cast: Where the Dunder Mifflin folks are now​.
shorts.description: Over ten years since the last episode, ‘The Office US’ is still one of the all-time favorites. This mockumentary about the oddball staff at Dunder Mifflin in Scranton, Pennsylvania, first hit screens in 2005 and stuck around for nine seasons, wrapping up in 2…

shorts.title: The AI boom has plunged a small Pennsylvania town into chaos
shorts.title_distilled: AI boom has plunged a small Pennsylvania town into chaos.
shorts.description: Data centers will swallow 14 percent of Archbald, evict a trailer park, and border many residential properties. Who's to blame is a matter of fierce debate.
shorts.content: Data centers will swallow 14 percent of Archbald, evict a trailer park, and border many residential properties. Who's to blame is a matter of fierce debate.

shorts.title: The Pennsylvania Town Facing a Data Center Boom
shorts.title_distilled: Pennsylvania Town Facing a Data Center Boom.
shorts.description: Archbald, Pennsylvania, a borough of fewer than 8,000 people, may soon be home to five massive data centers that, when completed, will rank among the largest in the world. While residents are worried that data centers will strain the electric grid and drive u…

shorts.title: Heavy police presence on scene in Scranton
shorts.title_distilled: Heavy police presence on scene in Scranton.
shorts.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — Police were out Tuesday evening investigating an incident in Scranton. Officers say they responded to reports of shots fired at a home on Clay Avenue in Scranton around 6:30 p.m. About a dozen police officers were seen on Costello Court behind the house, and caution tape blocked the alleyway. The [...]

shorts.title: Lawmakers hold public reading on move to ban kratom products
shorts.title_distilled: Lawmakers hold public reading on move to ban kratom products.
shorts.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — Scranton lawmakers held a first public reading on Tuesday of a controversial move to ban kratom products in the Electric City. Several people who say they've benefited from the pure version of kratom spoke out. They say it's not fair to lump the synthetic version of the over-the-counter substance with [...]

shorts.title_distilled: Emergency shelters open after Code Blue declared.
shorts.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — The recent weather means it is going to be dangerous outside Tuesday night in Lackawanna County, and officials want vulnerable people off the streets. shorts.title: Scranton road becomes one-way amid Streetscape Project
shorts.title_distilled: Scranton road becomes one-way amid Streetscape Project.
shorts.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — Heads up if you drive near Tripp Park in Scranton: Euclid Avenue is now a one-way street into the neighborhood as part of a west side streetscape project. That means drivers cannot turn from Euclid onto Main Avenue. Drivers are told to use Farr Street or Court Street instead. Work [...]
shorts.content: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — Heads up if you drive near Tripp Park in Scranton: Euclid Avenue is now a one-way street into the neighborhood as part of a west side streetscape project. That means drivers cannot turn from Euclid onto Main Avenue. Drivers are told to use Farr Street or Court Street instead. Work [...]

shorts.title: Justin Schuback life sentence upheld in Baron murder case
shorts.title_distilled: Justin Schuback life sentence upheld in Baron murder case.
shorts.description: (WBRE/WYOU) — The state's highest court has shut the door on Justin Schuback's appeal, cementing his life sentence for the murder of Old Forge business owner Robert Baron. Schuback had appealed a State Superior Court's October decision, which upheld his conviction. 58-year-old Robert Baron, owner of Ghigiarelli's Pizzeria, disappeared in 2017. His body was found [...]
